\section{Timeline (auto-generated from git)}
\input{generated/timeline}

\section{Narrative notes}

\paragraph{2026-06 --- Data acquisition complete.}
The full FFVL CFD archive (seasons 1999--2000 to 2025--2026) has been scraped: about
203{,}000 flight records, of which $\sim$186{,}000 carry a GPS track. All
186{,}052 available tracks were downloaded. This is the \emph{first} source; data
collection is ongoing (XContest, and possibly other sites, via the same scraping approach).
\emph{What worked:} impersonating a Chrome TLS fingerprint (\texttt{curl\_cffi}) reliably
passes the FFVL Cloudflare challenge; the resumable, atomic, validated download survived
many interruptions without corruption.
\emph{What did not / gotchas:} 173 tracks could not be obtained --- 172 because the server
returns something that is not a valid IGC (broken or placeholder links on FFVL's side, so
a retry does not help), and 1 due to a transient Cloudflare challenge (worth a later
retry). On macOS/exFAT, the OS scatters \texttt{.\_*} sidecar files next to every written
file; a \texttt{clean} step removes them.

\paragraph{2026-06 --- Integrity verified.}
A full on-disk re-scan (\texttt{verify}) confirmed that all 186{,}052 downloaded
files are structurally valid IGC: no truncated files, no leftover \texttt{.part}, no read
errors. The verification logic was promoted from a throwaway script to a first-class
\texttt{soaring-ffvl verify} subcommand (reusing the download-time validator), with tests.

\paragraph{2026-06 --- Robustness of the data path.}
The external disk remounted under a different name (\texttt{HDD\_DISANTE} $\to$
\texttt{SSD\_DISANTE}), which would have silently broken a hard-coded path. Fix: removed
every machine-specific path from the repo (placeholder + \texttt{SOARING\_FFVL\_DATA\_ROOT}
environment variable) and added a start-up guard that aborts with a clear, actionable
message when \texttt{data\_root} is unset/unmounted --- and verified that \texttt{/Volumes}
is not user-writable, so a wrong path can never cause a silent download to the wrong place.

\paragraph{2026-06 --- Documentation.}
Project docs are built with MkDocs and published on GitHub Pages. Initially deployed with
\texttt{mkdocs gh-deploy}, which accumulated one built-site commit per deploy on a
\texttt{gh-pages} branch; migrated to the official GitHub Pages \emph{artifact} workflow
(build $\to$ upload artifact $\to$ deploy), removing the branch and keeping the git history
clean.

\paragraph{Operational note.} In this repo \texttt{uv sync} occasionally fails to
(re)install the editable package; \texttt{uv sync --reinstall-package soaring} fixes it.

\section{Next steps}
\begin{itemize}
  \item Extend data collection beyond FFVL (XContest, possibly other sites) via scraping,
        and merge the sources into the same catalog.
  \item Pre-process IGC \texttt{B} records into clean, resampled, quality-filtered
        trajectories in a local metric frame.
  \item Re-examine the transition/search/climb segmentation of Vilpellet et al. (HMM on
        binary features: sign of vertical speed, curvature-angle persistence, straightness)
        --- assess whether those variables hold on our larger dataset and improve if needed;
        then extract step lengths, waiting times, search durations and per-phase statistics.
  \item After segmentation, reproduce Vilpellet et al.\ on the enlarged dataset as a
        validation + universality check: per-phase conditional MSD (their Fig.~3) and
        per-phase descriptive statistics (their Fig.~4), plus the global $H\approx0.88$.
  \item Estimate mean-squared-displacement scaling and the step/waiting-time
        distributions; test for anomalous transport.
  \item Minimal stochastic model for $H\approx0.88$: resolve transition segments, merge
        search+climb into one effective state, with angular diffusion between successive
        transition directions (preliminary idea, to be refined/changed).
  \item Refresh \texttt{data/seasons\_index.csv} via \texttt{build-catalog} so the public
        document's statistics stay current.
\end{itemize}

\end{document}
